channel.title: EMERGENZ Biosecurity Intelligence Dashboard
channel.description: High-severity biosecurity signals and recent authority/media coverage. Generated 2026-06-21T09:39:00.801Z.

item.title: [ACTION] Ebola disease — Bundibugyo virus, Democratic Republic of the Congo
item.description: WHO has declared the Bundibugyo ebolavirus outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, with cross-border spread to Uganda. As of June 11, 2026 (Brown Pandemic Center Tracking Report, sourcing WHO), the DRC has reported 635 confirmed cases and 127 confirmed deaths (CFR ~20%), across 25 affected health zones in Ituri, North Kivu, and South Kivu. Uganda has reported 19 confirmed cases (2 confirmed deaths, 1 probable death) — a total of at least 654 cases and 130 deaths across both countries. Contact-tracing compliance is above 80% in North/South Kivu but only 43% in Ituri. Nakivale refugee camp (Southwest Uganda, population ~150,000) identified 13 suspected cases, all quarantined. The regional response has escalated: Uganda border health measures, a contested US-supported quarantine/care arrangement in Kenya, a Brazilian investigation of possible imported cases, and accelerated multi-candidate vaccine efforts. These figures are updated from WHO-derived reporting and are subject to retrospective revision — verify the latest WHO situation report before operational use.

item.title: [CONCERN] Andes virus hantavirus cluster — MV Hondius travel-linked
item.description: Andes hantavirus cluster linked to a South America–Antarctica expedition cruise aboard MV Hondius. 13 reported cases (12 confirmed, 1 probable) and 3 deaths across multiple countries, with multinational case-finding and post-exposure monitoring continuing.

item.title: [CONCERN] Measles — United States
item.description: CDC confirmed 2,104 measles cases in 2026 as of June 18 — the highest US annual total since 1991 — across 41 jurisdictions and 30 outbreaks, with 93% of cases outbreak-associated. The 2026 total has surpassed the full-year 2025 total (2,074 cases). No deaths reported in 2026. U.S. kindergartener MMR coverage has declined to 92.5% (from 95.2% in 2019–2020), leaving approximately 286,000 kindergarteners unvaccinated — the structural driver of sustained outbreak activity.

item.title: [CONCERN] Mpox — clade I, Africa & travel-linked importations
item.description: Mpox clade I and travel-linked importation monitoring remain operationally significant for clinical recognition, specimen routing, and laboratory clade differentiation. Madagascar reports 3,241 total cases (1,976 confirmed) and 7 deaths as of June 9, up 6.5% since May 31. Mauritius confirmed its first locally acquired mpox case (June 4, 2026). Reunion reports 15 total 2026 cases (3 new, including 2 locally acquired and 1 imported from Madagascar — presumed clade Ib). Hong Kong reports 2 new cases linked to a sauna cluster (5 epidemiologically connected). Pakistan (Lahore) has 54 cumulative cases (predominantly clade Ib). Verify current country-level trends and any importation details against specific Africa CDC, WHO, or CDC updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Cholera — sub-Saharan Africa, including Republic of Congo
item.description: Africa CDC reported 40,707 cholera cases continent-wide from January 1 through May 14, 2026 — a 65% year-over-year decline compared to the same period in 2025. Despite the overall volume decrease, case fatality ratios remain elevated in conflict-affected and low-healthcare-access settings. Sudan, Ethiopia, Somalia, Mozambique, Malawi, and Zimbabwe continue reporting active transmission.

item.title: [CONCERN] Chikungunya — global outbreak activity
item.description: Chikungunya surveillance remains active across parts of the Americas and other mosquito-active regions. A notable new focus is French Guiana, with 513 confirmed cases through June 2026, prompting a CDC Level 2 Travel Health Notice (June 4, 2026). Brazil reports 86,970 total cases (32,691 confirmed) through mid-2026. Verify current country burden and case totals against specific PAHO or WHO updates before publication.

item.title: [CONCERN] Yellow fever — Colombia (Tolima) & the Americas
item.description: PAHO reports sustained yellow fever transmission across parts of South America, with cases expanding beyond traditional Amazonian risk zones. The department of Tolima, Colombia — where human cases had not been seen in over a century — is the current epicenter and concentrates the highest case and death counts in the country. Transmission is driven overwhelmingly by unvaccinated individuals. As of the week of June 11, the Colombia outbreak has stabilized with no new cases or deaths reported in the most recent week (Brown Pandemic Center, June 11 2026). CDC has issued Level 2 Travel Health Notices for Colombia and Venezuela. Verify current national figures against PAHO and Colombia's Instituto Nacional de Salud (INS) before publication; counts are revised as cases are laboratory-confirmed.

item.title: Google News: Thirty-eight Filipino crewmembers of MV Hondius, which was hit by an outbreak of Andes hantavirus during voyage, returned to the country on Friday after completing a 42-day quarantine in the Netherlands. http://tiny.cc/8se5101 - facebook.com

item.title: BBC Health: Jeremy Clarkson in remission from prostate cancer
item.description: The presenter shared his "aggressive" cancer diagnosis on an episode of Clarkson's Farm earlier this week.
